title: Farewell to Ravelrig by James Thomson
content:
Sweet Ravelrig, I ne'er could part
From thee, but wi' a dowie heart.
When I think on the happy days
I spent in youth about your braes,

title: A Poem Sacred to the Memory of Sir Isaac Newton by James Thomson
content:

title: Hymn on Solitude
content:
Hail, mildly pleasing Solitude,
    Companion of the wise and good,
    But from whose holy piercing eye
    The herd of fools and villains fly

title: Rule, Britannia!
content:
When Britain first, at Heaven's command,
    Arose from out the azure main,
    This was the charter of the land,
    And guardian angels sung

title: Fareweel, ye bughts
content:
Fareweel, ye bughts, an' all your ewes,

title: The Vine
content:
The wine of Love is music,
    And the feast of Love is song:
    And when Love sits down to the banquet,
    Love sits long:
